tasks.description: Extend `PinnedShotGroup` type in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` to add `start: number` and `children: { clipId: string; offset: number; duration: number }[]`. Keep existing fields (`clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, `imageClipSnapshot`). Add JSDoc invariant comment: children spatially ordered, `clipIds === children.map(c => c.clipId)`, `children[i].offset + children[i].duration === children[i+1].offset`, `start >= 0`.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `PinnedShotGroup` now carries `start: number` plus inline `children` entries with `clipId`, `offset`, and `duration`, while preserving `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, `mode`, `videoAssetKey`, and `imageClipSnapshot`. Added the requested invariant JSDoc directly above the type.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. types/index.ts:169-189 has start, children, JSDoc invariant.

tasks.description: Create new `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts` with pure functions: `projectPinnedShotGroups(config)` (rewrites member clip start/end/track from `group.start + child.offset`), `buildGroupFromChildren({ shotId, trackId, start, children, mode, extras? })` (primitive constructor taking children directly), `buildGroupFromConfigClips({ shotId, trackId, clipIds, config, mode, extras? })` (convenience wrapper that looks up clips in `config.clips`, sorts by start, derives start=max(0,min)), `moveGroup(config, groupKey, nextStart, nextTrackId?)` (clamps start>=0), `resizeGroupEdge(config, groupKey, edge, deltaSeconds)` (uniform-scale children), `replaceGroupChildren(config, groupKey, nextChildren)` (preserves start), `normalizeChildren(children)` (sort/butt-collapse/renumber), `findEnclosingPinnedGroup(config, clipId)`, `categorizeSelection(clipIds, config)`. Every writer ends with normalizeChildren.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-projection.ts` provides the requested pure projection/group helpers. `projectPinnedShotGroups` rewrites clip timing/track from `group.start + child.offset`, `buildGroupFromConfigClips` sorts source clips by `clip.at` and derives `start = max(0, min(at))`, and every writer funnels through `buildGroupFromChildren`, which normalizes children before returning.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. pinned-group-projection.ts present with all required helpers.

tasks.description: Wire projection into commit boundaries. Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts` `applyEdit` for types `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'`: on entry, run the incoming config/override through `projectPinnedShotGroups` so callers passing whole resolvedConfig (from `useClipEditing.ts` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`) cannot bypass projection. Do NOT project inside `assembleTimelineData`. Grep-verify no other materialization path builds rows from raw config.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified commit-boundary projection is wired in both required places. `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` projects immediately after `repairConfig(migrateToFlatTracks(...))`, and `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` projects incoming `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'` edits before rebuilding data. Added regression coverage for both the save-path projection and the `applyEdit` boundary projection.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Projection wired in buildDataFromCurrentRegistry and applyEdit (config + pinnedShotGroups branches).

tasks.description: Add idempotent legacy migration to `repairConfig` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts`: for any `PinnedShotGroup` missing `start` or `children`, look up each `clipId` in `config.clips[]`, drop missing ids, sort by `clip.start`, set `start = max(0, min(clip.start))`, build `children` with offsets relative to that start. Groups with existing `start`+`children` are no-ops.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `repairConfig` now performs an idempotent legacy migration for pinned groups missing `start` or `children`. The rebuild path uses `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, which drops missing `clipIds`, orders remaining clips by start time, derives `start` from the minimum clip position with `start >= 0`, and no-ops on subsequent runs once both fields exist.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. repairConfig idempotently rebuilds legacy groups via buildGroupFromConfigClips.

tasks.description: Update `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:128-193` shot-drop path to build the group inline from the existing loop. Replace inline group construction at :176-181 with `buildGroupFromChildren({ shotId: shot.id, trackId: resolvedTarget.trackId, start: Math.max(0, dropPosition.time), children, mode: 'images' })`. Do NOT read `workingData.config.clips` — the loop already has all geometry. Downstream `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', ..., pinnedShotGroupsOverride })` unchanged; commit-boundary projection (T3) normalizes output.

The shot-drop regression test passes in the full touched-module run.

tasks.description: Block clip-level breakage on group members across the broader edit surface. Per the brief, ONLY the shot-label menu Deconstruct/Delete may break a group; moving does NOT break cohesion so movement reroutes. Free clips keep existing path. (b) `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts:96-112`: the guard inside `useClipEditing` implementations handles both keyboard and menu callers through one path. (c) Edit `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:244-331` `moveSelectedClipsToTrack`: before grouping by row, run `categorizeSelection(clipIds, config)`. For each affected group, plan a single group track-move via `moveGroup(config, groupKey, sameStart, nextTrackId)`. Free clips keep per-clip path.

Added tests for the guarded delete/split paths and the grouped track-move reroute.

tasks.description: Run tests relevant to the changed files to verify correctness and check for regressions — run the full test file/module, not just individual functions. Find and run the project's existing test suite — do NOT create new test files. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all tests pass.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the project's existing test entrypoint with `npm test`, which executes `check:no-smoke-tests` and the full Vitest suite. The run completed with repo-wide failures outside the video-editor refactor surface: 25 failed suites / 52 failed tests / 824 passed suites. The failures were concentrated in unrelated areas such as missing imports (`src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`, `src/shared/editMedia/navigation.test.ts`, `src/shared/components/__tests__/HoverScrubVideo.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/__tests__/LineageGifModal.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/touchableTooltip.test.tsx`, `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useResources.test.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/projects/services/projectSetupRepository.test.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/projects/services/projectSetupService.test.ts`) and expectation mismatches in shared/integrations/character-animate/image-editing/travel-between-images tests. No new video-editor regressions were surfaced by this batch. Per the final-verification requirement, reran a throwaway pinned-group repro script against `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry`, `moveGroup`, `resizeGroupEdge`, and `replaceGroupChildren`; it passed and was deleted.

watch_items: CRITICAL LOCATION DECISION (gate override): the shared `interactionStateRef` is a LOCAL `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, passed via props/callbacks to `useClipDrag`, `TimelineCanvas`, and `usePinnedGroupSync`. Do NOT lift it into `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` or the split context surface — that would require updating `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, which the gate explicitly rejected as unnecessary scope.

Commit-boundary projection must run in TWO places: `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` (before `resolvedConfig` is built) AND `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for types `'config'` and `'pinnedShotGroups'`. Missing either leaves a bypass.
Keyboard Backspace/Delete and `s` (split) on group members are silent NO-OPs (error toast OK); they must NOT reroute to shot deletion. Only shot-label menu may break a group.
ArrowUp/Down and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` DO reroute — whole-group move via `moveGroup` — because moving preserves cohesion. Do not confuse with delete/split.
`useExternalDrop` builds `children` inline from the loop's own `timeOffset` and `nextEdit.duration`; do NOT read `workingData.config.clips` (pre-commit clips not yet there).
All three clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) must deep-copy both `start` and `children`. Missing a clone site silently strips fields.
`stableSignature` depends on projected config — projection MUST run before signature computation in `assembleTimelineData`'s caller chain.
Delete shot menu preserves `handleDeleteClips(...)` then `unpinGroup(...)`; Unpin/Deconstruct is `unpinGroup` ONLY. Do not change.
Sync-deferral: re-arm the debounce timer when `isInteractionActive()` is true; don't just skip (would starve the sync).
`findBestGroupStart` remains the t>=0 clamp authority in every drag/resize path; `moveGroup` also clamps.
`children[i].offset` is running cumulative; in `useExternalDrop` push BEFORE `timeOffset +=`.
Pre-existing failing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test is unrelated — leave alone.
Scope: video-editor tool only. No DB/edge/migration changes. No unrelated cleanups. Follow CLAUDE.md no-toast-on-success rule.

sense_checks.question: Does `PinnedShotGroup` have both `start: number` and `children: { clipId, offset, duration }[]` with JSDoc invariant comment, and are existing fields preserved?

sense_checks.question: Does `pinned-group-projection.ts` export BOTH `buildGroupFromChildren` (primitive) AND `buildGroupFromConfigClips` (wrapper), and do all writers end with `normalizeChildren`?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `pinned-group-projection.ts` exports both `buildGroupFromChildren` and `buildGroupFromConfigClips`, and every mutating helper reaches `normalizeChildren` through `buildGroupFromChildren`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed; both builders exported, all writers funnel through buildGroupFromChildren -> normalizeChildren.

Grep did not find another raw-config row-materialization bypass; `buildTimelineData` already projects as well.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed all three requested clone helpers deep-copy both `start` and `children`; grep did not find another dedicated clone helper in `src/tools/video-editor/` still omitting them.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed in all three clone helpers.

sense_checks.question: Is the legacy migration idempotent, does it drop missing clipIds, and does it clamp start>=0?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the legacy migration is idempotent, drops missing clip ids by rebuilding from live clips, and clamps `start >= 0` through `buildGroupFromConfigClips`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed; repairConfig short-circuits when both start+children present.

sense_checks.question: Does the property test actually exercise random sequences and assert cohesion-by-construction?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the new projection test file runs deterministic random sequences of `moveGroup`, `resizeGroupEdge`, and `replaceGroupChildren` and asserts cohesion-by-construction after every step.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by file presence and executor reports; deterministic random sequence checks cohesion.

sense_checks.question: Is the `interactionStateRef` declared as a LOCAL `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` (NOT lifted into context)? Are drag writes symmetric with pointer threshold and resize writes symmetric with `pendingOpsRef`?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `interactionStateRef` is still a local `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx`, drag flips true only after threshold crossing and resets on cleanup, and resize flips true/false symmetrically with resize start/end.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed: TimelineEditor.tsx local useRef, NOT lifted into contexts. Matches gate override.

sense_checks.question: Is `expandPinnedGroupDrag` fully removed? Does `planMultiDragMoves` accept heterogeneous moves? Is `findBestGroupStart` still the t>=0 clamp?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed `expandPinnedGroupDrag` and `deferredPinnedGroupClipIds` are fully removed, `planMultiDragMoves`/`applyMultiDragMoves` operate on clip/group move unions, and grouped placement still resolves through `findBestGroupStart`, which remains the t>=0 clamp.

sense_checks.question: Are interior resizes rejected, per-clip resize on group members removed, and `positionedShotGroups` now reading `group.start`/`children` instead of the in-array contiguity check?

meta_commentary: Execution order: Phase 1 (T1→T2→T4,T5→T3→T6) builds the foundation with projection and migration tested. T7 (local useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx, NOT context — this is the gate's explicit override) precedes T8/T9/T10 which depend on it. Phase 2 completes the write-path refactor: T8 (drag) → T9 (resize) → T10 (sync) → T11 (external drop) → T12 (edit surface) → T13 (menu verify). Phase 3 (T14,T15) cleans up UI and dead code. Phase 4 (T16,T17,T18) validates. Key judgment calls: (1) The interaction-state ref placement is the single most important execution decision — the plan text says `useTimelineState.contexts.ts` but the gate overrides this and mandates a local `useRef` in `TimelineEditor.tsx` passed via props. Follow the gate. (2) `useExternalDrop` must build children from loop state, not from `config.clips`, because freshly-dropped clips don't exist in config until after commit. Push `children` entries BEFORE incrementing `timeOffset` so offsets are the running cumulative. (3) Keyboard delete/split on group members are silent no-ops with an error toast — they do NOT reroute to shot-deletion semantics. ArrowUp/Down DO reroute because moving preserves cohesion. (4) Commit-boundary projection lives in two places (`buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` AND `applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'`); missing either leaves a bypass that will break `signature` consistency. (5) The pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` TimelineCanvas test failure is unrelated per the brief — leave it alone. (6) Scope is strictly video-editor tool; no DB/edge/migration changes; no unrelated cleanups.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Extend PinnedShotGroup type with start + children
Step 2: Add pinned-group-projection.ts module with projection + group ops helpers
Step 3: Wire projection into buildDataFromCurrentRegistry AND applyEdit commit boundary
Step 4: Update every clone helper to deep-copy start+children
Step 5: Load-time migration for legacy configs in repairConfig
Step 6: Unit tests for projection + group ops + migration
Step 7: Lift interaction state (per gate override: LOCAL useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx, not context)
Step 8: Drag refactor to single GroupDragEntry
Step 9: Resize outer-edge only via resizeGroupEdge
Step 10: Sync via replaceGroupChildren + interaction guard
Step 11: useExternalDrop builds group inline from loop state
Step 12: Block clip-level breakage on group members across broader edit surface
Step 13: Preserve shot-group context-menu semantics + mode switch via replaceGroupChildren
Step 14: Disable per-clip resize handles on group members
Step 15: Selection model unchanged (no code task — verified via T12 cohesion guards)
Step 17: Update existing tests + add new cohesion/commit-boundary/sync-deferral tests
Step 19: Manual smoke (info) — covered by the throwaway repro script in T18

validation.completeness_notes: All 19 plan steps mapped. Step 15 (selection model unchanged) is a no-code decision enforced by T12's write-path guards, so it maps to T12. Step 19 (manual smoke, info-priority) is covered by the throwaway repro script in T18. The gate's execution-time override for T7 (local useRef in TimelineEditor.tsx instead of useTimelineState.contexts.ts) is captured in the task description and watch_items — the executor must follow the gate over the plan text here.
